meta.purpose: Encode expert judgment for using GSAP as a production animation engine, including timelines, ScrollTrigger, React integration, plugin choice, and performance tradeoffs.
meta.load_condition: Load when the task involves GSAP implementation, timeline architecture, ScrollTrigger, or when the user is working within a React/Vue/Svelte context using GSAP.

axioms.one_sentence: Use timelines to express choreography, not scattered one-off tweens.
axioms.full_statement: Use timelines to express choreography, not scattered one-off tweens.
axioms.why: Timelines preserve sequence, reversibility, maintainability, and reviewability.
applies_when: Building multi-step UI transitions like form validation feedback sequences
Creating synchronized marketing hero animations with staggered element reveals
Implementing interactive tutorials with ordered step-by-step highlights
Animating data visualization updates that require precise timing relationships between bars or lines
does_not_apply_when: Hover or focus micro-interactions that trigger instantly and reset independently
Dynamic list item animations where each item reacts to separate user clicks asynchronously
Physics-based or real-time simulation updates driven directly by requestAnimationFrame
Simple on-demand state changes like showing or hiding a tooltip
axioms.failure_risk: Creating an overly rigid, hard-to-modify animation structure for independent or user-driven events that should trigger and complete on their own schedule.

axioms.one_sentence: Framework integrations must clean up animations according to component lifecycle.
axioms.full_statement: Framework integrations must clean up animations according to component lifecycle.
axioms.why: Leaked animations cause duplicated effects and broken state after navigation.
applies_when: React components unmounting during client-side route navigation
Vue components being conditionally destroyed with v-if toggles
Angular components dismantled after modal closures or tab switches
Reusable animation wrappers that mount and unmount frequently in virtualized lists
does_not_apply_when: Singleton page-level animations that persist across the entire application session
Server-side rendered initial load animations that fire once before hydration
Global scroll-triggered sections attached directly to the main application shell
Standalone demo files or codepens that lack framework component lifecycles
axioms.failure_risk: Over-cleaning persistent global animations or prematurely killing long-running background effects, causing visual glitches or lost application state.

axioms.one_sentence: ScrollTrigger should guide reading, not hijack it.
axioms.full_statement: ScrollTrigger should guide reading, not hijack it.
axioms.why: Scroll-driven motion can easily make content feel trapped or gimmicky.
applies_when: Highlighting key statistics or quotes as the user naturally scrolls past them
Revealing content sections progressively to reduce cognitive load on long articles
Adding subtle parallax backgrounds that add visual depth without distorting text layout
Displaying progress indicators that reflect the reader's current position
does_not_apply_when: Pinning full-screen overlays that force users to scroll multiple times just to continue reading
Triggering heavy CPU-intensive animations that cause layout shifts or frame drops during scrolling
Disabling native scroll behavior to enforce a forced vertical-to-horizontal scroll experience
Animating critical UI controls or navigation elements out of view while the user scrolls
axioms.failure_risk: Creating an inaccessible, frustrating reading experience that prioritizes visual flair over content delivery and user control.

axioms.one_sentence: A dropped frame destroys premium perception.
axioms.full_statement: A dropped frame destroys premium perception.
axioms.why: Smoothness is part of visual quality, not an implementation detail.
applies_when: Marketing landing pages for luxury or tech brands where micro-interactions define brand identity
Frequently used UI component transitions (e.g., navigation menus, modal open/close) that shape perceived responsiveness
Hero section scroll-triggered animations that immediately establish first impressions on high-traffic pages
does_not_apply_when: Background decorative elements or off-screen parallax layers where frame drops are imperceptible to the user
Accessibility contexts where reduced-motion preferences are active, prioritizing static clarity over fluid tweening
Legacy device or low-bandwidth fallback states where aggressive GSAP timelines should be disabled rather than forced to 60fps
Internal operational dashboards where rapid data load times and functional density outweigh animation polish
axioms.failure_risk: The agent will ship choppy transitions in high-visibility UI paths, causing users to perceive the product as cheap or untrustworthy despite correct functional implementation.

ontology.one_sentence: A structured animation sequence with coordinated tweens and labels.
ontology.essence: A structured animation sequence with coordinated tweens and labels.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'timeline' or describes this scenario.

ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'ScrollTrigger' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: A controlled offset across similar elements to create rhythm and hierarchy.
ontology.essence: A controlled offset across similar elements to create rhythm and hierarchy.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'stagger' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: Removing or reverting animation side effects when a component unmounts or re-renders.
ontology.essence: Removing or reverting animation side effects when a component unmounts or re-renders.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'cleanup' or describes this scenario.

Do not use scroll animation when it reduces reading comfort.
